response_json.brief_summary: Keep the page as a support guide on official Gift Center login and UID setup, with a narrow first-screen clarification that strengthens route validation without changing cluster role or adding a new player job.
response_json.primary_user_job: Confirm that Gift Center setup, UID usage, and official service routing stay accurate.
response_json.first_screen_plan: Preserve the answer-first layout. Make the opening answer explicitly validate the official browser-based Gift Center flow, UID copy path, and mailbox reward delivery in a way that reinforces the existing support-guide role. Keep the same template wrapper, class names, and route labels. Do not broaden into a general redemption or store-guide page.

section_plan.action: Tighten the lead so the first screen clearly states the official browser flow, UID copy path, and mailbox outcome.
section_plan.reason: This is the highest-value place to answer the user job quickly while staying within the existing page role.

section_plan.action: Keep the step order focused on official login, UID copy, browser redemption, and reward delivery.
section_plan.reason: The user needs a precise validation flow, not a new topic or a generic help article.

section_plan.action: Retain and, if needed, slightly sharpen the warnings about wrong UID entry, in-app redemption, and checking the mailbox.
section_plan.reason: These are the main failure points for the existing user job.

section_plan.action: Ensure the checks map directly to the official flow and can be scanned quickly on mobile.
section_plan.reason: Supports fast validation without altering the page structure.

section_plan.action: Keep internal routing toward start, f2p, codes, and redeem-code-not-working as the next logical paths.
section_plan.reason: Preserves cluster separation and helps users branch to adjacent intent pages.

internal_link_plan.reason: Keeps the page anchored in the main site hub and preserves existing information architecture.
Useful for players who need onboarding after confirming the Gift Center flow.
Relevant follow-on guidance for players evaluating progression and economy context.
Adjacent but distinct intent for players looking for code lists rather than setup validation.
Useful fallback when the official flow does not work as expected.
Potential supporting guide destination if the site uses it for related help content.

do_not_change: Do not publish or apply content changes from this brief automatically.
Do not change the existing page template, navigation pattern, or schema family without separate approval.
Do not create a new page when the Scout proposal recommends updating an existing page.
Do not use analytics signals as proof that a rewrite is required.
The topic duplicates an existing page intent without adding a distinct player job.
The external claim cannot be verified beyond this source.
The proposal would blur existing cluster roles.
owner_questions: Do you want the first screen to emphasize official routing verification more strongly, or should it stay almost unchanged aside from minor clarity edits?
Is `resources.html` still a valid related destination for this cluster, or should it be removed from the internal link plan?
Should we keep the page title as a pure setup/login framing, or is a subtle clarity tweak approved for the first-screen heading only?
acceptance_checks: manual review: first-screen answer and internal links on gift-center-uid.html

exact_replacements.exact_old: <meta name="description" content="Official Last Z Gift Center login setup: how to copy your UID from Avatar > Settings > Copy ID, redeem in a browser, and collect rewards from mailbox.">
exact_replacements.exact_new: <meta name="description" content="Official Last Z Gift Center login setup: how to copy your UID from Avatar > Settings > Copy ID, redeem in a browser, and collect rewards from your in-game mailbox.">
exact_replacements.reason: Aligns the meta description with the mailbox claim already used on-page and improves route clarity without changing page role.

response_json.next_step: Verify the current file against canonical claims and nearby cluster pages, then decide whether only the first-screen and meta description need narrow approval-ready edits or whether the page should stay unchanged.

safety: No content, backlog, manifest, or production files were modified by the LLM adapter.
